result:
All 20 usage sites of `sig.qual`/`sig.noise` are in place across `wpa_supplicant/bss.c`, `scan.c`, `events.c`, and `src/drivers/driver_bsd.c`, `driver_nl80211_scan.c`, `driver_wext.c`, while the unrelated `struct wpa_bss` fields (`bss->qual`/`bss->noise` in `ctrl_iface.c`/`dbus_old_handlers.c`) and Linux `iw_event`/`iw_statistics` fields were correctly left untouched.

Summary:
- `struct wpa_scan_res` in `src/drivers/driver.h` now has `struct { int qual; int noise; } sig;` replacing the old `qual`/`noise` fields (doc comment updated too).
- Updated all 9 call sites across `wpa_supplicant/bss.c`, `scan.c`, `events.c`, `src/drivers/driver_bsd.c`, `driver_nl80211_scan.c`, and `driver_wext.c` to use `->sig.qual`/`->sig.noise`.
- Verified the full `wpa_supplicant` build compiles and links cleanly (exit 0) after working around this macOS sandbox's missing Linux headers (used `CONFIG_TLS=internal`, `CONFIG_INTERNAL_LIBTOMMATH`, `CONFIG_L2_PACKET=freebsd`, `CONFIG_OSX=y`, disabling `CONFIG_DRIVER_WEXT`/`CONFIG_DRIVER_NL80211` only for the local test build since those source files need real Linux kernel headers unavailable here â€” I manually verified those two files' edits by reading the exact struct types at each site instead). Build artifacts and the temporary `.config` were cleaned up afterward.
